source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A cord passing over a frictionless pulley has a 9 kg mass tied on one end and a 7 kg mass on the other end (Fig. 1.2). Determine the tension of the cord.
extraction_notes: image fields contain PIL object representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: `requires_image` is false, so the problem is explicitly not image-required.
The full task is solvable from the text alone: two masses on a frictionless pulley with a requested tension.
`text_dominant` is true, which further indicates low reliance on the attached image.
multi_step: Solving typically requires setting up the coupled motion of a two-mass pulley system rather than reading off a fact.
A standard solution combines at least two relations or stages, e.g. find acceleration from both masses and then compute tension.
The reasoning is still fairly short and formulaic, so multi-step demand is moderate rather than high.
verifiability: The expected answer is a single numeric short-text value (`77.2 N`), which is easy to compare against derived results.
The physics setup is well-specified in text (masses and frictionless pulley), enabling rule-based or symbolic checking.
`answer_present` is true and `registry_passed` is true, so the sample metadata is complete and reliable for evaluation.

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
the problem is text-solvable and retains complete core semantics even without relying on the image
high verifiability because the expected answer is a short numeric result and the setup is well specified
only minor risk is numeric rounding tolerance, which does not materially reduce candidate value

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A cord passing over a frictionless pulley has a 9 kg mass tied on one end and a 7 kg mass on the other end (Fig. 1.2). Determine the tension of the cord.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image fields contain PIL object representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs
metadata.reasoning_chain: Neglecting the moment of inertia of the pulley, we obtain the equations of motion $m_{1} \ddot{x}=m_{1} g-F$ and $m_{2} \ddot{x}=F-m_{2} g$. Hence the tension is $$F=\frac{2m_{1}m_{2}g}{m_{1}+m_{2}}=77.2 \mathrm{~N}$$
